qa_pairs.question: How do I create model checkpoints in TensorFlow when using Keras callbacks?
qa_pairs.answer: To create model checkpoints during training with Keras, you should use the `callbacks` parameter of the `model.fit()` method. For example, to create a checkpoint at the end of every training epoch, you can use `callbacks = [tf.keras.callbacks.ModelCheckpoint(filepath="./ckpt", save_freq="epoch")]`. Ensure the specified `filepath` exists.

qa_pairs.question: What is the solution for the `OMP: Error #15` when `libiomp5.so` is already initialized, especially when using scikit-image with TensorFlow?
qa_pairs.answer: This error occurs because the TensorFlow library attempts to load its own bundled version of OpenMP (`libiomp5.so`), which conflicts with an already initialized system version. The workaround involves navigating to your virtual environment directory (e.g., `tf_skimage_venv`) and executing specific commands to patch the TensorFlow installation to use the systemwide `libiomp5.so`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the known performance issue with Keras `tf.keras.layers.Random` data augmentation layers in TensorFlow, and which versions are affected?
qa_pairs.answer: A bug in the Keras implementation of TensorFlow after version 2.8.3 significantly slows down the training process, by more than 100 times, when using data augmentation layers with the prefix `tf.keras.layers.Random` (e.g., `tf.keras.layers.RandomRotation`, `tf.keras.layers.RandomTranslation`). This bug has since been fixed in version 2.12.
